summary: Gallai's 1966 conjecture that any three longest paths in a connected graph share a common vertex remains open in general. Meaningful partial results confirm the conjecture for specific graph classes (series-parallel, outerplanar, split, $H$-free graphs with linear-forest $H$), and a 2025 paper improves the best known bound on the minimum vertex set hitting all longest paths to $O(\sqrt{n})$. An attempted general proof (arXiv:2006.16245, 2020) was withdrawn by its author as erroneous.

since_posted.title: Non-Empty Intersection of Longest Paths in $H$-Free Graphs
since_posted.authors: James A. Long Jr., Kevin G. Milans, Andrea Munaro
since_posted.claim: Characterizes all graphs $H$ on at most 4 vertices for which every connected $H$-free graph has a single vertex meeting all longest paths; these are exactly the linear forests, confirming Gallai's property for those graph classes.

since_posted.title: Small hitting sets for longest paths and cycles
since_posted.authors: Sergey Norin, Raphael Steiner, Stephan Thomassé, Paul Wollan
since_posted.claim: Proves every connected $n$-vertex graph has a set of at most $\sqrt{8n}$ vertices intersecting all longest paths, improving the previous $O(n^{2/3})$ state-of-the-art bound.

search_queries: Gallai conjecture three longest paths common vertex connected graph
longest paths intersection Gallai conjecture graph theory recent results 2013 2024
Skupien longest paths empty intersection Gallai problem progress
notes: arXiv:2006.16245 (Sarkar, 2020) claimed a full proof but was withdrawn as erroneous by the author. The Discrete Math. 2022 paper on chordal graphs (DOI 10.1016/j.disc.2022.113035) returned HTTP 403 and could not be verified; not cited. arXiv:2310.03849 (Gutiérrez–Valqui, 2023) focuses on Smith's and Hippchen's conjectures about pairwise cycle/path intersections in $k$-connected graphs, not the 3-path Gallai conjecture.
